title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
summary: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that provides multiple development surfaces: Antigravity 2.0 desktop app, IDE, CLI, and SDK. It enables developers to build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents that can autonomously perform development tasks by connecting to code editors, terminals, and browsers.
writeup: This video maps the four development surfaces in Google Antigravity, an agent-first software development platform, and clarifies when to use each: the Antigravity 2.0 desktop app, the IDE, the CLI, and the SDK. It matters because the platform centers on building, orchestrating, and monitoring AI agents that autonomously perform development tasks by connecting to code editors, terminals, and browsers. The takeaway is that each surface targets a different entry point into the same agent workflow, so the choice depends on whether work happens in a desktop app, an editor, the terminal, or through programmatic integration.
